findings.message: get_item has no return path when the item is found. After the `if not item:` branch raises, execution falls off the end of the function and returns None, violating the `-> Item` annotation and `response_model=ItemRead` (FastAPI will fail to serialize None against ItemRead, producing a 500 on every successful lookup).
findings.suggestion: Add `return item` after the HTTPException branch so the found Item is actually returned.

findings.message: Using a falsy check (`if not item`) to test for a missing row is incorrect in general. SQLAlchemy ORM instances are normally truthy, but relying on truthiness conflates None with any object whose __bool__ could be False; the intent is strictly 'row not found'. Use an explicit identity check.
findings.suggestion: Replace `if not item:` with `if item is None:` to express the not-found condition unambiguously.
